findings.evidence: `DegreeSplitting.lean:113` defines `degreeCorrection`, `DegreeSplitting.lean:118` proves `degreeCorrection_apply`, and the new theorem at `AbelJacobiSumBasepointChange.lean:66-72` uses only `degreeCorrection_apply`, `weightedDegreeClass`, and `pointDifference`, all available before `AbelJacobiSum`/`BasepointChange`; grep shows its only consumers are the later divisor-sum lemmas in the same new file (`:93`, `:103`, `:153`).
findings.fix: Move `degreeCorrection_change_base` to `TauCeti/AlgebraicGeometry/WeilDivisor/DegreeSplitting.lean` near `degreeCorrection_apply`/`degreeCorrection_divisorClass`, then import/use it from this file for the divisor-sum specializations.
findings.issue: `degreeCorrection_change_base` is a general theorem about `OrderSystem.degreeCorrection`, not about Abel-Jacobi divisor sums, so placing it in the later `AbelJacobiSumBasepointChange` file hides broadly useful degree-splitting API behind divisor-sum imports.

summary: One public class-level theorem is placed in the new divisor-sum basepoint-change file even though it belongs with the degree-correction API it elaborates.
